terms.definition: A neural network trained on large text corpora to model language—predicting continuations—that powers assistants like Claude after alignment.
A subword unit the model reads and writes; prompts and responses are measured and billed in tokens on the API.
The maximum number of tokens the model can attend to in one request, including instructions, attachments, and the reply being generated.
The dominant neural architecture family for modern LLMs, using attention layers to relate tokens across the input sequence.
Running a trained model on a new prompt to produce output; weights are fixed unless you switch to a newer model release.
Model-generated content that sounds plausible but is false or unsupported—a consequence of probabilistic text generation, not database lookup.
